ext_desc.message: Capture screenshots, video covers, Hooks, and keyframes to Obsidian or Notion

welcome_sub.message: Screenshots · Video covers · Hooks · Keyframes → straight into Obsidian

welcome_sub_obsidian.message: Screenshots · Video covers · Hooks · Keyframes → straight into Obsidian

welcome_sub_notion.message: Screenshots · Video covers · Hooks · Keyframes → straight into your Notion workspace

welcome_conn_ok_detail.message: All set — you're ready to go.

welcome_conn_bad_detail.message: Check: ① Obsidian is running ② the vault-autopilot plugin is installed and enabled ③ ports match (this extension uses $PORT$). This page turns green automatically once it's set up.

welcome_dest_notion.message: Notion (your workspace, using your own token)

welcome_notion_stage1_title.message: Create a connection and copy its token

welcome_notion_stage1_outcome.message: Create a connection, then copy its token.

welcome_notion_stage1_step2.message: Click “New connection” (or “新建连接” in a translated interface).

welcome_notion_stage1_step3.message: Enter any connection name you like. Remember the exact name because you will search for it in Stage 2.

welcome_notion_stage1_step6.message: On the Configuration page, find “Access token” and click the copy icon at the right of the field.

welcome_notion_stage1_step7.message: Save the copied token somewhere private. You will paste it into Visual Clipper in Stage 3.

welcome_notion_token_warning.message: Keep this token secret. Anyone who has it can use the connection's permissions. Never place it in a public note, screenshot, message, or code repository.

welcome_notion_img1_alt.message: Notion Connections page with the New connection button highlighted

welcome_notion_img1_caption.message: Reference 1: click “New connection” on the Connections page.

welcome_notion_img2_alt.message: New connection dialog with connection name and Create connection button highlighted

welcome_notion_img2_caption.message: Reference 2: name the connection, keep Access token selected, then create it.

welcome_notion_img3_alt.message: Notion connection Configuration page with the access-token copy button highlighted

welcome_notion_img3_caption.message: Reference 3: copy the Access token from the connection's Configuration page.

welcome_notion_stage2_title.message: Duplicate the template and add your connection

welcome_notion_stage2_outcome.message: We have prepared a ready-to-use Notion template for you. Click the button below to copy it to your Notion.

welcome_notion_stage2_step1.message: Open the template with the button above.

welcome_notion_stage2_step2.message: Choose “Duplicate as template” (or “复制为模板”) to copy the template to your Notion.

welcome_notion_stage2_step3.message: Open the template page you just copied. Do not configure the original public template.

welcome_notion_stage2_step4.message: On your copied template page, click the ••• menu in the top-right corner.

welcome_notion_stage2_step5.message: Open “Connections” or “Add connections” (translated interfaces may show “连接” or “添加连接”).

welcome_notion_stage2_step6.message: Search for the exact connection name from Stage 1, then click it once to add it to this page.

welcome_notion_stage2_step7.message: Copy this duplicated template page's link from Share or from the browser address bar. You will paste it in Stage 3.

welcome_notion_img4_alt.message: Copied Notion template showing the top-right menu, Connections row, and connection search field

welcome_notion_img4_caption.message: Reference 4: open •••, choose Connections, search for the Stage 1 name, and click the matching connection.

welcome_notion_stage3_title.message: Paste both values, save, and test

welcome_notion_stage3_outcome.message: Visual Clipper will store the settings locally and verify the connection token.

welcome_notion_token_helper.message: Paste the Access token copied in Stage 1. The field stays masked.

welcome_notion_parent_helper.message: Open the template you just copied, copy the link from your browser's address bar, and paste it here.

welcome_notion_bad.message: 🔴 Check the token, confirm the copied template has this connection, then try again

welcome_notion_invalid_parent.message: That doesn't look like a Notion page link

welcome_notion_tryit_body.message: Open any video, click Visual Clipper, then choose “Save cover”. A new or updated database page will open in Notion.

welcome_notion_tryit_link_text.message: Open YouTube and choose a video →

welcome_notion_workflow_title.message: How Visual Clipper works with Notion

welcome_notion_workflow_body.message: Visual Clipper captures content in your browser and sends it directly to the Notion workspace you authorized. The token stays in Chrome's local extension storage and is used only for your selected Notion destination.

welcome_notion_storage_body.message: Visual Clipper saves your captures in the Notion template you copied.

welcome_notion_storage_fact1.message: The first time you save a video or webpage, a new record is added to the template.

welcome_notion_storage_fact2.message: When you save the same link again, Visual Clipper updates the existing record instead of creating a duplicate.

welcome_notion_storage_fact3.message: Visual Clipper automatically fills in the title, source link, platform, and capture date.

welcome_notion_modes_title.message: Four ways to save into Notion

welcome_notion_mode_ss.message: Select a region: upload the selected image into the Notion page for that source URL.

welcome_notion_mode_cover.message: Save cover: create or update the video's database page with its cover and available metadata.

welcome_notion_mode_hook.message: Hook analysis: add the video embed, time range, selected frames, and transcript to the Hook section.

welcome_notion_mode_kf.message: Video keyframes: add a video embed at the selected start time, the time range, and selected frames to the Keyframes section.

welcome_notion_tip1.message: After a successful capture, Visual Clipper opens the corresponding Notion page.

welcome_notion_tip2.message: Keep the selected connection attached to the copied template or target page.

welcome_notion_tip3.message: This destination needs an internet connection because it writes through Notion's API.

welcome_notion_tip4.message: If the token is revoked or page access changes, return here and repeat the affected setup stage.

welcome_conn_bad_notion.message: 🔴 Notion not connected — fill in the token and page below

welcome_install_s2_html.message:
Unzip it into your vault: <your vault>/.obsidian/plugins/
Can't see .obsidian? It's hidden — press Cmd+Shift+. in Finder (macOS) or enable “Hidden items” in Explorer (Windows).

welcome_install_s3_html.message: Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins → enable Vault Autopilot. If “Community plugins” itself is switched off, turn it on first.

welcome_install_s4.message: Come back to this page — the status above turns green automatically 🟢

welcome_install_brat.message: Alternative: install with the BRAT plugin (auto-updates) — in BRAT choose “Add Beta Plugin” and enter github.com/echore/vault-autopilot

welcome_tryit_body.message: Open any video, click the extension icon, then hit “Save cover” — your first note lands in Obsidian in seconds.

welcome_tryit_link_text.message: Open YouTube and pick any video →

welcome_coop_title.message: How the two pieces work together

welcome_coop_body.message: The extension captures — screenshots, video frames and subtitles, inside your browser. The vault-autopilot plugin stores — it listens on local port 17183 inside Obsidian, writes the files into your vault and formats the note. Everything runs on your own machine; your content never passes through an external server.

welcome_config_hint.message: Zero configuration needed. To change storage folders, attach analysis SOPs, or change the port: Obsidian → Settings → Vault Autopilot.

welcome_storage_body_html.message: Works with zero config. One video = one note: save the cover, hooks and keyframes of the same video and everything appends into the same note, in any order. To change locations: Obsidian → Settings → Vault Autopilot → storage locations.

welcome_tree.message:
Clips/
├── Videos/            video notes (one per video)
│   ├── covers/        cover images
│   └── frames/        hook / keyframe stills
└── Screenshots/       web screenshot notes
    └── frames/        screenshot images

welcome_mode_ss_html.message: Select any region: click the toolbar icon → Screenshot → drag over the page → saved as a screenshot note.

welcome_mode_cover_html.message: Save the cover: on a YouTube / Bilibili video page click “Save cover” → grabs the cover image + title metadata → into the video note.

welcome_mode_hook_html.message: Capture the opening: on a video page click Hook analysis → samples candidate frames from the start up to the current position → pick the keepers in a grid → saved with the transcript into the video note.

welcome_mode_kf_html.message: Mark In / Out: click Keyframes on a video page → mark start and end in the side panel → pick the keepers in a grid → into the video note.

welcome_tip1.message: · After a save you jump straight to the note in Obsidian (the first time, tick “Always allow” in the “Open Obsidian” prompt to skip it later).

welcome_tip2_html.message: · Browser-restricted pages (chrome:// …) can't be region-selected; screenshot mode saves the full page instead and tells you in the popup.

welcome_tip3.message: · Keep Obsidian open while clipping — the extension delivers content to the plugin over a local port, so nothing can be saved while Obsidian is closed.

welcome_adv_summary.message: Advanced: port (only change on a port conflict)

welcome_adv_body_html.message: The extension and the plugin talk over a local port, default 17183. If the plugin reports the port is taken and you can't close the other program, set the same new port here and in the plugin settings (Advanced → Port).

popup_conn_bad_notion.message: Notion not connected · click for details

popup_hook_tracked.message: Tracking 0:00 ~ $T$ — click to capture up to here

sp_need_video.message: Open a video page to use this feature

sp_step1_body.message: Seek to where the segment starts, then mark it

sp_step2_body.message: Play on to the end point, then capture

ct_drag_hint.message: Drag to select a region · ESC to cancel

notif_notion_saved_body.message: Click to open the saved page.

ct_no_frame_capture.message: This video doesn't support frame capture

ct_video_not_ready.message: The video isn't ready yet (buffering or playing an ad) — let it play a few seconds and try again

ct_picker_title.message: Choose frames to keep — click to deselect the ones you don't want

connect_fail.message: Can't reach Obsidian: make sure Obsidian is running and the vault-autopilot plugin is enabled. Open “Setup guide / Help” at the bottom of the extension popup for troubleshooting steps.

err_page_restricted.message: This page is restricted by the browser and can't be captured

err_cannot_capture_refresh.message: Can't capture on this page — refresh and try again

err_data_lost.message: Screenshot data was lost — try again

err_no_response.message: vault-autopilot didn't respond — make sure Obsidian is running and the plugin is enabled

notice_fullpage.message: Region select isn't possible on this page, so the full page was saved instead.

err_cover_none.message: No cover image found on this page

err_cover_update.message: Saving covers requires updating the vault-autopilot plugin

err_cover_failed.message: Saving the cover failed — try again

err_page_comm.message: Can't communicate with the page — refresh and try again

err_kf_no_in.message: No In mark found — mark it again

err_kf_too_close.message: In and Out are too close together — mark again

err_notion_not_configured.message: Notion isn't set up yet — open the extension guide page to add your token and page link.

err_notion_auth.message: Notion rejected the token — generate a new one in Notion, then update it on the guide page.

err_notion_target.message: Notion page or database not found — re-select the page on the guide page and try again.

err_notion_rate.message: Notion is rate-limiting requests — wait a moment and try again.

err_notion_network.message: Can't reach Notion — check your network connection.

err_notion_bad_schema.message: That Notion database has no URL-type property — add a column of type URL to it (any name), or paste a plain page link instead.
